steps.title: Use the roots to write the factored form
steps.content: Since the graph of $f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$ passes through $(7, 0)$ and $(-3, 0)$, these are the roots. I can write: $$f(x) = a(x - 7)(x + 3)$$

steps.title: Expand and identify the coefficient b
steps.content: Expanding the factored form: $$f(x) = a(x - 7)(x + 3) = a(x^2 - 4x - 21) = ax^2 - 4ax - 21a$$ Comparing with $f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$, we have $b = -4a$.

steps.title: Determine which choice is possible for a + b
steps.content: Calculate $a + b$: $$a + b = a + (-4a) = -3a$$ Since $a$ is an integer greater than 1, $a + b = -3a$ is negative. Testing values: when $a = 2$, $a + b = -6$; when $a = 3$, $a + b = -9$. Only $-6$ appears in the choices. That matches choice A — A is correct.
